title: Earth, Environment, and Space Practice Test

questions.questionText: What do we breathe in from the air?
questions.explanation: We breathe in oxygen from the air. It is essential for life.

questions.questionText: Which planet do we live on?
questions.explanation: We live on Earth. It is the only planet with life.

questions.questionText: What causes day and night?
questions.explanation: Earth spins on its axis. When our side faces the Sun it is day.

questions.questionText: Which is a source of fresh water?
questions.explanation: Rivers are a source of fresh water. Seas and oceans have salty water.

questions.questionText: What do we call the layer of air around the Earth?
questions.explanation: The atmosphere is the layer of air that surrounds the Earth.

questions.questionText: How many seasons are there in a year?
questions.explanation: There are four seasons: spring, summer, autumn, and winter.

questions.questionText: What falls from clouds when it rains?
questions.explanation: Rain drops fall from clouds when water vapor condenses.

questions.questionText: Which is the closest star to Earth?
questions.explanation: The Sun is the closest star to Earth. It is a ball of hot gas.

questions.questionText: What should we do to keep our environment clean?
questions.explanation: Reduce, reuse, and recycle help keep our environment clean.

questions.questionText: Which direction does the Sun rise from?
questions.explanation: The Sun rises in the East and sets in the West.

questions.questionText: What do plants need from the air?
questions.explanation: Plants use carbon dioxide from the air to make food.

questions.questionText: Which is the largest ocean on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean on Earth.

questions.questionText: What do we use a map for?
questions.explanation: A map helps us find places and understand locations.

questions.questionText: When do we see the Moon?
options: At night and sometimes during day
questions.explanation: We usually see the Moon at night. Sometimes we see it during the day too.

questions.questionText: What is pollution?
questions.explanation: Pollution is when harmful substances dirty our air, water, or land.

questions.questionText: Which season is usually the hottest?
questions.explanation: Summer is usually the hottest season of the year.

questions.questionText: What shape is the Earth?
questions.explanation: Earth is round like a ball. It is called a sphere.

questions.questionText: Why should we save water?
options: Fresh water is limited and precious
questions.explanation: Fresh water is limited. We should save it for ourselves and future generations.

questions.questionText: What do we call moving air?
questions.explanation: Wind is moving air. We can feel it on a windy day.

questions.questionText: Which is a natural resource?
questions.explanation: Wood from trees is a natural resource. We get it from nature.

questions.questionText: How long does Earth take to go around the Sun once?
questions.explanation: Earth takes about 365 days (one year) to orbit the Sun.

questions.questionText: What do clouds contain?
questions.explanation: Clouds contain tiny drops of water or ice crystals.

questions.questionText: Which helps reduce air pollution?
questions.explanation: Trees absorb carbon dioxide and give us oxygen. They help reduce pollution.

questions.questionText: What is a globe?
questions.explanation: A globe is a round model of the Earth. It shows land and water.

questions.questionText: Which season is usually the coldest?
questions.explanation: Winter is usually the coldest season of the year.
